content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 12, 1994

Sally Holshouser
Santee Recall 1994
P.O. Box 711033
Santee, CA 92072

Re: Your Request for
Confirmation of Telephone
Advice
Our File No. I-94-115

Dear Ms. Holshouser:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹ Since your request is one for general
information, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).²

Your letter dated March 30, 1994, correctly summarizes my
advice. Loans made to committees may be repaid and the Act
imposes no limit on contributions, including loans, which are made
to committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall
measures. A non-monetary loan made to a committee which is not
repaid in the same reporting period in which it is made must be
reported on Schedule C as a non-monetary loan, and also on
Schedule F as an unpaid bill owed to the lender. For more
complete instructions, see page 30 in the enclosed Information
Manual D.

The Act requires city clerks and other filing officers to
provide FPPC forms and manuals to candidates and committees to
enable them to satisfy their filing requirements under the Act.
Please contact the Technical Assistance Division of the Fair
Political Practices Commission for technical information regarding

_______________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Holshouser, Sally
I-94-115
Page 2


     the Act, at the number given below. If your city clerk is not
cooperative in providing information or materials necessary for
you to comply with your filing obligations, please contact us.

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Wayne H. Imberl
                                        Political Reform Consultant


Enclosures

sections.conclusion: Loans made to committees may be repaid and the Act imposes no limit on contributions, including loans, which are made to committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures. A non-monetary loan made to a committee which is not repaid in the same reporting period in which it is made must be reported on Schedule C as a non-monetary loan, and also on Schedule F as an unpaid bill owed to the lender.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure requirements for loans made to committees formed to qualify, support, or oppose recall measures, and how should non-monetary loans be reported?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act imposes no limit on contributions or loans to recall measure committees, and loans may be repaid. Non-monetary loans not repaid in the same reporting period must be reported on both Schedule C (as a non-monetary loan) and Schedule F (as an unpaid bill).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone advice. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly present in body text. Document explicitly states this is informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c) and does not provide immunity of formal opinion.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure requirements for loans made to committees formed to qualify, support, or oppose recall measures, and how should non-monetary loans be reported?

CONCLUSION: Loans made to committees may be repaid and the Act imposes no limit on contributions, including loans, which are made to committees formed to qualify, support or oppose recall measures. A non-monetary loan made to a committee which is not repaid in the same reporting period in which it is made must be reported on Schedule C as a non-monetary loan, and also on Schedule F as an unpaid bill owed to the lender.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Sally Hoishouser regarding campaign disclosure requirements for loans to committees supporting or opposing recall measures, clarifying that there are no contribution limits and specifying the reporting requirements for non-monetary loans.
